prevalence.trend: stable; burden driven by smoking and biomass exposure

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to chronic respiratory disability

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Spirometry with airflow limitation and imaging findings; differentiate from chronic bronchitis

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis based on symptoms and spirometry
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; limited by spirometry access and inhaler adherence

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Spirometry access mainly in urban centers; imaging for emphysema in tertiary hospitals
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Smoking cessation, inhaled bronchodilators, pulmonary rehab, vaccinations; oxygen for advanced disease
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High biomass exposure and limited spirometry access in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing COPD programs and availability of essential inhalers
